tweet: In the meantime, Congress has the power, and the constitutional duty, to set the size of the Court, as it has 7 times throughout our history.  My colleagues and I need not wait for the findings of a commission. We know the obvious: we must #ExpandTheCourt before it’s too late.

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: Many Americans will rightly be skeptical of a commission composed almost entirely of people protected from the real-life consequences of SCOTUS's right-wing extremism.   Nevertheless, I remain hopeful that the commission will join our rising movement for Court expansion.

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: The answer to that question is clear: to restore our democracy, we must expand the Supreme Court.   Anything less would leave the future of our nation, our planet, and our fundamental civil rights at the whim of a far-right supermajority that is hostile to democracy itself.

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: It’s the prerogative of the legislative branch to determine the size of the Supreme Court, not the Article III judges themselves.

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: A7: In communities with small-donor matching programs, candidates spend more time engaging everyday people about the issues that matter to them and less time asking wealthy donors for money.  This is about creating a government that is truly by and for We, the People. #S1TownHall

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: A6: Congress isn’t just funded by wealthy people — it’s full of them. This is because our broken campaign finance system is rigged to favor wealthy candidates.  Small-donor financing gives power back to the people so they can help elect the candidates that truly represent them.

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: To build a true democracy, we need to make sure that our politicians listen to us, the people — not just their biggest donors.   With small-donor matching, candidates who have historically been excluded from electoral politics can run people-powered campaigns and win. #S1TownHall

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: A4: The small-donor matching program in #S1 doesn’t use a single taxpayer dollar. Instead, the program is funded entirely by fines and penalties paid by big corporations who break the law. It’s a win-win. #S1TownHall

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: A3: Small-donor matching is simple: for every dollar a person donates up to $200, public financing matches that dollar with $6.   That means a $50 donation becomes a $350 donation, giving small-dollar donors the power to make a real difference in our elections. #S1TownHall

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: Too often, big-dollar donors drive the outcome of so many elections, helping the wealthy and well-connected control our government.   By establishing public campaign financing, the #ForThePeopleAct would help elect more working people, women, people of color, and young people.

name: Rep. Mondaire Jones
tweet: A1: As part of the most diverse Congress in U.S. history, I know we're close to creating an inclusive, equitable democracy.   But we're not there yet. We need a system that empowers people of color, working people, and everyone else our politics has excluded. A true democracy.
